text.title: Documentation Core Rules
text.summary: 5 hard rules for the AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md pair.

rule.statement: Every source-containing directory has BOTH AGENTS.md (content) and CLAUDE.md (= `@AGENTS.md`).
rule.rationale: Claude Code reads CLAUDE.md; other agents read AGENTS.md; the pair satisfies both.

rule.statement: AGENTS.md is 20-80 lines (hard cap 120). Past 120 the auto-load budget is wasted on prose.
rule.rationale: Auto-load context costs tokens on every agent invocation; the cap forces signal density.

rule.statement: AGENTS.md has these sections: title + 1-line purpose + file table (≥3 cols: file / kind / why) + key commands + gotchas + (optional) related.

rationale.source: Internal — observed parse-failure cases without structure

rule.rationale: Predictable structure lets agents parse without reasoning.

rule.statement: If a README.md exists, it MUST link to AGENTS.md from the first paragraph; never duplicate its content.

rationale.source: Internal — observed drift in mediamanager docs

rule.rationale: Two sources of truth diverge; the link keeps humans + agents pointed at one place.

rule.statement: Any PR that adds / renames / removes a file in a dir MUST update that dir's AGENTS.md file table in the same PR.
rule.rationale: Stale tables are worse than missing ones; tying the update to the PR keeps them honest.
